  "findings": [
    "Missing dielectric union or approved transition fitting between copper and steel piping",
    "Galvanic corrosion hazard due to direct contact of dissimilar metals"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Cut out the direct copper-to-steel connection",
    "Install an approved dielectric union, brass valve, or brass coupling to isolate the dissimilar metals"
  ],
  "rationale": "Connecting copper directly to galvanized steel or iron piping without an approved dielectric transition creates a galvanic cell, leading to rapid corrosion and premature joint failure. This violates standard plumbing codes (such as IPC Section 605.24) and manufacturer installation instructions.",
  "references": [
    "IPC Section 605.24 (Joints Between Different Materials)",
    "Manufacturer installation instructions for copper and steel piping transitions"
  ]
}
